name: Ruta rosa, Parque National de Monfragüe – circular
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Malpartida De Plasencia offer diverse terrain at the gateway to Monfragüe National Park. The region is characterized by extensive dehesas of holm oaks, Mediterranean forests, and significant water bodies like the Embalse de las Covachillas. Riders can explore routes that traverse these varied landscapes, encountering both gentle paths and more challenging sections. The area also features historical elements and hills such as Cerro de los Castillejos, providing varied elevation for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Malpartida De Plasencia The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta rosa, Parque National de Monfragüe – circular, a 26.4 miles (42.4 km) trail that takes about 3 hours to complete. This moderate route winds through the Dehesa Boyal El Robledo and past natural reservoirs. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Monfragüe Greenway – Bazagona Greenway loop from Malpartida de Plasencia, a moderate 26.9 miles (43.3 km) path. This route offers scenic views of the Monfragüe area and its unique ecosystems. Local mountain bikers also love the Church of San Juan Bautista loop from Malpartida de Plasencia, a 11.4 miles (18.4 km) trail leading through varied local landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 40 minutes. Mountain biking around Malpartida De Plasencia is defined by its dehesas, Mediterranean forests, and the natural features of Monfragüe National Park. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Malpartida De Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Malpartida De Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find around Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: The region offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from easy paths suitable for beginners to more challenging routes for experienced riders. You'll find trails winding through extensive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; of holm oaks, Mediterranean forests, and alongside significant water bodies. Overall, there are &lt;strong&gt;6 easy&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;13 moderate&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;5 difficult&lt;/strong&gt; mountain bike routes available.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, there are several options for families and beginners. The area features &lt;strong&gt;6 easy trails&lt;/strong&gt; that are generally less demanding. These routes often traverse the scenic &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; and offer a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region.

Q: What natural features and landscapes will I encounter on the trails?
A: Mountain biking around Malpartida De Plasencia immerses you in a rich natural environment. You'll ride through expansive &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; (wooded pastures) with ancient holm oaks, Mediterranean forests, and encounter various water bodies like the Embalse de las Covachillas and Pantano del Horco del Espino. The region is also a gateway to Monfragüe National Park, known for its dramatic rock formations and diverse ecosystems.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to see along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the trails often pass by points of historical and cultural interest. You can explore sites like the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers breathtaking views, or the small settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4458065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarreal de San Carlos&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features ancient fountains scattered throughout the &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; and hills like Cerro de los Castillejos with remains of a Vetton settlement.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: The mountain bike trails in Malpartida De Plasencia are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;4.5 out of 5 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural surroundings, and the opportunities to explore the unique landscapes of the Monfragüe area.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops, making them convenient for starting and ending in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013828166/ruta-rosa-parque-national-de-monfraguee-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta rosa, Parque National de Monfragüe – circular&lt;/a&gt;, a 42.4 km trail that winds through the Dehesa Boyal El Robledo and past natural reservoirs.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While specific regulations can vary, Monfragüe National Park, which borders the area, has strict rules regarding pets to protect its wildlife. It's advisable to check local park guidelines or specific trail rules before bringing your dog, especially within protected areas. Many &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; trails outside the core park areas may be more accommodating.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or scenic spots along the trails?
A: The region offers numerous scenic viewpoints. The ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/429392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Castle&lt;/a&gt; provide panoramic views of the National Park. You can also find scenic spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3767067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, offering stunning vistas of the reservoirs and surrounding landscapes.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Malpartida De Plasencia and nearby access points to the trails typically offer parking facilities. For routes venturing into Monfragüe National Park, designated parking areas are usually available at visitor centers or trailheads, such as those near Villarreal de San Carlos.

Q: What is a good moderate trail for mountain biking in the area?
A: A great moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31475676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monfragüe Greenway – Bazagona Greenway loop from Malpartida de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;. This 43.3 km path offers scenic views of the Monfragüe area and its unique ecosystems, making it a popular choice among local mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6910618" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro Gimio – Villarreal de San Carlos loop from Malpartida de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and longer distances. This particular route is rated as difficult, with over 1100 meters of elevation gain.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Malpartida De Plasencia?
A: The best time to go mountain biking is generally during the spring (late March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Spring is particularly beautiful with blooming flora, and the famous cherry blossoms in the nearby Jerte Valley (late March/early April) add to the regional appeal.
tips: Toril is a nice little town with just a few streets, but it's a good place to start an adventure on the surrounding trails. An interesting thing to see is the Hermitage of San Blas.
When there is water it becomes a beautiful place to stop and take a break, watch birds and enjoy nature.
